design_note: This is a memoir braided with a self-help framework, and the Sydney 2000 final is the spine that earns every principle — so the navigation is strictly linear: the sequence IS the argument, and theme-clustering would collapse the build of dread, doubt, and finally release that the chronology does. We use both illustration channels because the book operates on two registers at once. Channel B openers carry the felt experience (dawn water, winter ergs, chalk on a boathouse wall) so the reader arrives in scene; Channel A diagrams externalise each chapter's framework (decision filters, 2x2s, causal chains) so the self-help half is load-bearing, not decorative. Muted watercolour — ochre, slate, paper texture — fits the tone: this is a quiet, deliberate, British rowing memoir, not a cinematic sports thriller. Wet edges and restrained line work read as recollection, which is the voice the prose is already in.
navigation_rationale: The Olympic-final narrative builds chronologically across all twelve chapters; the framework only lands because the story keeps moving forward.

channel_b_opener.prompt: the morning of the Sydney 2000 Olympic men's eight rowing final, calm water, dawn light, eight rowers in white singlets carrying a long racing shell to the dock, distant grandstands, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: Sydney 2000, morning of the men's eight final

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart TD
  Dream[Crazy dream] --> Crazy{Is it crazy enough\nto matter?}
  Crazy -->|yes| Realistic{Is it realistic\nenough to commit?}
  Crazy -->|no| Bigger[Make it bigger]
  Realistic -->|yes| Goal[Crazy-realistic goal]
  Realistic -->|no| Refine[Find the realistic path]
  Bigger --> Crazy
  Refine --> Realistic
channel_a_diagram.caption: Goals framework: the crazy-realistic dream test

channel_b_opener.prompt: a lone rower on an ergometer in a dim boathouse before sunrise in winter, breath visible in cold air, single hanging bulb, frost on the windows, an open training logbook on a bench, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: Winter ergo session before dawn — the daily showing-up

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart LR
  Trigger[Alarm at 5am] --> Friction{Friction\nlow enough?}
  Friction -->|no| Reduce[Lay kit out night before\nPack bag\nBike by door]
  Friction -->|yes| Action[Get to the boathouse]
  Reduce --> Action
  Action --> Reward[Log session\nTick the day]
  Reward --> Identity[I am someone\nwho shows up]
  Identity --> Trigger
channel_a_diagram.caption: Motivation loop: lower friction, log the rep, reinforce identity

channel_b_opener.prompt: a rower sitting alone on a wooden bench in an empty boathouse, head down, hands loose, an old framed photograph of past GB crews on the wall behind, afternoon light through high windows, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: Sitting with a limiting belief in an empty boathouse

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart TD
  Event[Event or setback] --> Belief[Belief about it]
  Belief --> Feeling[Feeling]
  Feeling --> Action[Action]
  Action --> Result[Result]
  Result -.reinforces.-> Belief
  Belief --> Check{Is this belief\nhelpful and true?}
  Check -->|no| Swap[Choose a more\nhelpful belief]
  Swap --> Belief
channel_a_diagram.caption: Beliefs drive results — and beliefs are a choice

channel_b_opener.prompt: a coach's whiteboard in a rowing club office covered with crossed-out times and rewritten splits, a mug of tea cooling beside it, afternoon shadows, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: A whiteboard of stories being interrogated

channel_a_diagram.caption: Bullshit filter: three questions before a story gets to run you

channel_b_opener.prompt: a rower stepping off a dock into a single scull at first light, water glassy and still, oars resting on the rigger, a faint mist over the river, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: Choosing to push off — making it happen

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart LR
  Wake[Wake up] --> Choose{Choose:\nwhat kind of day\nwill this be?}
  Choose --> Set[Set one intention]
  Set --> Act1[First action\nbefore 9am]
  Act1 --> Momentum[Small win]
  Momentum --> Act2[Next action]
  Act2 --> Evening[Evening:\nwas it a good day?]
  Evening -->|yes| Bank[Bank it]
  Evening -->|no| Reset[Reset tomorrow]
channel_a_diagram.caption: Make-it-happen sequence: today is a choice, not a forecast

channel_b_opener.prompt: an eight-person racing shell on a river seen from above, all eight rowers and the cox in perfect rhythm, oars catching the water as one, wake fanning out symmetrically, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: An eight in rhythm — a team performing as one

channel_a_diagram.content:
quadrantChart
  title Team roles: clarity vs trust
  x-axis Low clarity --> High clarity
  y-axis Low trust --> High trust
  quadrant-1 High-performing crew
  quadrant-2 Friendly but confused
  quadrant-3 Dysfunctional
  quadrant-4 Efficient but brittle
  Bow pair: [0.85, 0.8]
  Engine room: [0.9, 0.9]
  Stroke and cox: [0.95, 0.85]
  New recruit: [0.4, 0.5]
channel_a_diagram.caption: Teams: clarity of role × trust in each other

channel_b_opener.prompt: the inside of a boathouse wall with the phrase 'will it make the boat go faster?' written in white chalk, a single oar leaning beneath it, late afternoon light, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: The title question, in chalk on the boathouse wall

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart TD
  Option[Any decision\nor invitation] --> Filter{Will it make\nthe boat go faster?}
  Filter -->|yes| Do[Do it]
  Filter -->|no| Drop[Don't do it]
  Filter -->|unclear| Test[Run a small test\nthis week]
  Test --> Filter
  Do --> Review[Review at end of week:\ndid it actually?]
  Review --> Filter
channel_a_diagram.caption: The one filter: every decision passes through the same question

channel_b_opener.prompt: a long racing shell already underway on open water, bow wave curling cleanly, oars mid-drive, a sense of accumulated speed, low sun behind, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: A boat carrying speed — momentum already paid for

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart LR
  Push[Hard work in] --> Speed[Boat speed]
  Speed --> Glide[Glide between strokes]
  Glide --> Next[Next stroke is easier]
  Next --> Push
  Drag[Drag: skipped sessions\nadmin\ndrama] -.removes.-> Speed
  Catch[Catch: protect the run\nbetween strokes] -.preserves.-> Glide
channel_a_diagram.caption: Momentum: the glide between strokes is where the boat lives

channel_b_opener.prompt: a rowing crew on a lake under fast-moving grey clouds, wind beginning to ripple the surface, a coach on the bank pointing toward shelter, kit bags being repacked, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart TD
  Signal[New signal:\ncondition has changed] --> Pause[Pause briefly]
  Pause --> Reread{What's still true?\nWhat just stopped\nbeing true?}
  Reread --> Goal{Does the goal\nstill stand?}
  Goal -->|yes| Replan[Same goal,\nnew plan]
  Goal -->|no| Restate[New goal,\nnew plan]
  Replan --> Move[Move]
  Restate --> Move
channel_a_diagram.caption: Change protocol: goal first, plan second

channel_b_opener.prompt: a single rower walking back along a dock in the rain after a lost race, oar over one shoulder, head up, a teammate waiting at the end of the dock with a towel, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: After a loss — the walk back

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart LR
  Setback[Setback] --> Feel[Feel it\n24 hours max]
  Feel --> Facts[Separate facts\nfrom story]
  Facts --> Learn[One lesson worth\nkeeping]
  Learn --> Next[Next concrete action\nthis week]
  Next --> Back[Back in the boat]
  Back --> Stronger[Stronger crew]
channel_a_diagram.caption: Bouncebackability: a bounded loop, not a spiral

channel_b_opener.prompt: a coach and a rower studying a course map spread on a wooden table outside a boathouse, weighted at the corners by stones, a mug of tea and a stopwatch nearby, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: Weighing risks over a course map

channel_a_diagram.content:
quadrantChart
  title Three risk categories
  x-axis Low cost of acting --> High cost of acting
  y-axis Low cost of NOT acting --> High cost of NOT acting
  quadrant-1 Can't afford NOT to take
  quadrant-2 Afford NOT to take
  quadrant-3 Ignore
  quadrant-4 Can't afford to take
  Change stroke rate mid-race: [0.7, 0.85]
  Try a new training block: [0.3, 0.7]
  Skip recovery to train more: [0.8, 0.25]
  Argue with the cox in the final: [0.9, 0.15]
channel_a_diagram.caption: Risk grid: cost of acting vs cost of not acting

channel_b_opener.prompt: the finish line at Penrith Lakes, Sydney 2000, the British men's eight crossing first, oars raised, exhausted faces, gold light on the water, a single GB flag in the crowd, STYLE: watercolor, muted ochre and slate palette, soft wet edges, restrained line work, paper texture visible
channel_b_opener.alt: Gold — the British men's eight across the line at Sydney 2000

channel_a_diagram.content:
flowchart TD
  Goal[1. Crazy-realistic goal] --> Motiv[2. Motivation]
  Motiv --> Belief[3. Helpful beliefs]
  Belief --> Filter[4. Bullshit filters]
  Filter --> Make[5. Make it happen]
  Make --> Team[6. Team]
  Team --> Process[7. Will it make\nthe boat go faster?]
  Process --> Mom[8. Momentum]
  Mom --> Change[9. Change]
  Change --> Bounce[10. Bounceback]
  Bounce --> Risk[11. Risk]
  Risk --> Gold[12. Cross the line]
  Gold -.next goal.-> Goal
channel_a_diagram.caption: The whole framework, in the order the race needs it

characters_json.Ben Hunt-Davis: GB rower in his 30s, lean rowing build, short brown hair, white singlet with GB lion crest, calm focused expression
characters_json.the crew: eight British rowers plus cox, white singlets with GB lion crest, racing shell on calm water, synchronised posture
characters_json.Sydney_2000: Penrith Lakes regatta course, Australian morning light, low grandstands along the bank, eucalyptus in the distance, calm water
